settings.text: Each batch is prepared over several days so the herbal essence fully binds to the oil instead of sitting on the surface.

settings.text: This is a botanical product, but patch testing first is still the safest place to start for sensitive scalps.

settings.heading: Key Ingredients
settings.milk_text: Three nourishing milks work together for a healthier scalp and stronger hair.
settings.steps_text: Use the oil two to three times a week with a consistent routine.

settings.title: Full ingredient list
settings.text:
Oil base
Sesame oil (Sesamum indicum), Coconut oil (Cocos nucifera)

Primary herbs
Neeli (Indigofera tinctoria), Bhringaraja (Eclipta alba), Amla (Emblica officinalis)

Triple-milk base
Coconut milk, Goat milk, Cow milk - a genuine differentiator in Ayurvedic hair oil formulation

Supporting herbs
Manjishtha, Yashtimadhu, Haritaki, Vibhitaki, Jatamansi, Padmaka, Sariva, Nilotpala, Durva, Gunjamoola, Priyangu, Anjana, and Lauha bhasma.

Processed over 7 days using traditional Taila Paka Vidhi.

settings.title: Preparation - 7 days
settings.text:
Preparation

Each batch follows the Sahasrayogam's Taila Paka Vidhi - a classical method requiring 7 days of slow-heat extraction. Herbs are first made into a kashaya, then combined with the sesame oil base and kalka. The mixture is heated on low flame until the water content fully evaporates and the herbal essence binds to the oil.

settings.title: Safety & interactions
settings.text:
Safety & interactions

External use only. Always patch test before first full application. Pregnant or nursing? Talk to your doctor before starting a new topical regimen.

settings.title: Frequently asked
settings.text:
When will I see results?
Reduced hair fall is typically noticed within 2-3 weeks. Visible improvement requires consistent use.

Does it help with greying?
Neeli and Bhringaraja are traditionally used to support natural hair colour.

Can I use it on coloured hair?
Yes. The formula is gentle, but extended use may slightly darken hair over time.

Is it safe during pregnancy?
Consult your doctor before starting any new topical regimen.
